titleStmt.title: Letter from M.J. Burke to Sir Robert Chalmers, 1 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: M.J. Burke

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is addressed to Sir. Robert Chalmers from M. J. Burke. This letter concerns the appointment of members to serve on a Visiting Committee for Belfast prison. A draft list is enclosed with the letter.Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938) was sent to Ireland as replacement under-secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916. M. J. Burke was the Clerk of the Crown and Peace for Antrim and Belfast.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO-RP-1916-5615-11278

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
All letters to be addressed â
'Clerk of the Crown and Peace,
County Court House, Belfast.' County Court House, Belfast. 1st July, 1916. Sir, I beg to acknowledge receipt of your communications
of yesterday enclosing forms for return of the names of
the Justices to be appointed at the forthcoming Assizes
for the County of Antrim and City of Belfast, to serve as
members of the Visiting Committee of the Belfast Prison. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, M J Burke
Clerk of the Crown and Peace
for Antrim and Belfast.
Mr Donnelly
Please keep a record
as was done in 1914
3 -VII-16 Send reminder to as regards
Galiory Prison & as regards
Co. Clare member of Committee
of Limerick Prison.
10 . VIII. 16 The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
Antrim, 3 (12961) Belfast
Armagh, 6 (12312) Armagh
Carlow, 2 (12814) Kilkenny
Cavan, 1(12253) Dundalk: 2(12252) Armagh
Clare, 2 (14171) Limerick
Cork, E.R., 8 (12960) Cork MTF
Cork, W.R.2 (1417
Donegal, 2(12600) L. Derry

Dublin, Boro 8 (11448) Waterford Mjoy P.
Fermanagh, 2 (12812)
Galway, E.R.,
Galway, W.R., 10 (14172) Galway
Kerry, 8 (12845) Tralee
Kildare, 2 (12811) Kilkenny : 2 1 (12810) Mjoy
Kilkenny, 1 (12553) Kilkenny: 7 (13244) Kilkenny

Sligo, 5 (12384) Sligo: 2

Waterford Corp, 3 (11539) Waterford P
Westmeath,2 2 (12184) Tullamore
Wexford, 2 (12035) Waterford P.
Wicklow, 1 (12112) Mjoy P.

Belfast, 6 (12962) Bfast
